disjointness_evidence: 0 mechanistic cross-citations between geochemical glass dissolution (TST, PHREEQC) and pharmaceutical ASD dissolution (Noyes-Whitney, Higuchi). 21 PHREEQC+pharmaceutical results are all environmental contamination modeling, not dissolution prediction.

bridge_strength.mathematical_analogy: STRONG — parabolic rate law (sqrt(t)) independently derived in both fields for identical physics
bridge_strength.quantitative_framework: STRONG — TST rate law provides composition-based prediction absent in pharma
bridge_strength.tool_transfer: STRONG — PHREEQC has 0 pharmaceutical citations but handles exactly the relevant chemistry
bridge_strength.composition_prediction: STRONG — geochemistry has composition-dissolution rate functions; pharma has none

critical_gap_identified: No TST-equivalent predictive framework exists in pharmaceutical dissolution science. All current models are empirical (fit-to-data), not predictive from composition.
